signals.headline: ETH ETFs 8-Day Inflow Streak Confirmed | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
ETH ETFs 8-Day Inflow Streak Confirmed | YES | HIGH confidence**  
U.S. spot Ethereum ETFs saw $67.77 million net inflows on April 20 (8th consecutive day) and $276 million for the week, with Fidelity’s FETH leading at $126 million; ETH up 3.33% to $2,395.[1][3]
matched_markets: Ethereum Up or Down - April 23, 2:30AM-2:35AM ET

signals.headline: BTC ETFs 5-Day Inflow Streak Confirmed | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
BTC ETFs 5-Day Inflow Streak Confirmed | YES | HIGH confidence**  
U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $238.37 million in net inflows on April 20, extending a 5-day streak—the firmest institutional bid since February—led by BlackRock’s IBIT at $256.05 million.[1][3] This signals strong demand amid Bitcoin trading around $75,900-$78,497 (up 2-3.6%).[1][2]

signals.headline: Bitcoin $80K by April End Odds Jump | YES | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
Bitcoin $80K by April End Odds Jump | YES | MED confidence**  
Odds for Bitcoin hitting $80K in April 2026 have risen amid ETF momentum, tokenization gains, and prior short squeezes, with BTC breaking $75K resistance on institutional flows.[4][7] No new October or prediction market resolutions confirmed.[2]
